jamez-bondos/awesome-gpt4o-images ·GitHub ⭐ 7862 JavaScript · GPT-4o, Prompt-Engineering, Image-Generation, OpenAI, Generative-Art, Awesome-List

What it is

A curated awesome list of images and prompt examples generated by OpenAI's GPT-4o and gpt-image-1, demonstrating state-of-the-art AI image creation.

Problems solved

  • Helps users discover
  • learn
  • and apply advanced prompting techniques for GPT-4o
  • providing inspiring use cases ranging from surreal compositing to product designs and cartoon illustrations.

How to get started

Browse the categorized cases in the README. Each case provides the reference output alongside the exact Chinese/English prompts used, which you can adapt by swapping out variables like subject, style, and object.

Detailed report

1. Positioning

jamez-bondos/awesome-gpt4o-images is positioned as a a curated awesome list of images and prompt examples generated by openai's gpt-4o and gpt-image-1, demonstrating state-of-the-art ai image creation. It aims to provide developers with a robust, JavaScript-based solution for awesome curated collection of images and prompts generated by gpt-4o and gpt-image-1. explore ai generated visuals created with chatgpt and sora, showcasing openai’s advanced image generation capabilities.

2. Pain Points & Advantages

Traditional methods in this domain often suffer from Helps users discover. jamez-bondos/awesome-gpt4o-images addresses this by a curated awesome list of images and prompt examples generated by openai's gpt-4o and gpt-image-1, demonstrating state-of-the-art ai image creation, offering a significant performance boost and better developer ergonomics compared to existing alternatives.

3. Technical Architecture

The project is built using JavaScript, adopting a modular and scalable design. It leverages modern industry standards to ensure compatibility across different environments, focusing on efficiency and ease of integration.

4. Core Implementation

5. Code Organization

The repository is structured logically, with core functionality separated from utilities and examples. This organization facilitates easy navigation and contribution for developers looking to extend the tool's capabilities.

6. Quick Start Suggestion

To get started, browse the categorized cases in the readme. each case provides the reference output alongside the exact chinese/english prompts used, which you can adapt by swapping out variables like subject, style, and object. This will allow you to quickly evaluate the tool and integrate it into your existing development workflow.

7. Summary
